entry.title: Just in from the news desk 📰: What is TxGemma?

group.title: Just in from the news desk 📰: What is TxGemma?
group.description:
We're excited to release TxGemma, a collection of open models designed to improve the efficiency of therapeutic development by leveraging the power of large language models. See if this tool can be used in your developer projects.

entry.title: Day in the Life of a Google Wallet Tech Lead

group.title: Day in the Life of a Google Wallet Tech Lead
group.description:
Curious about what it's like to be at the forefront of Google’s digital wallet? Join us for an exclusive behind-the-scenes look into the daily life of Gokmen Goksel, a Tech Lead for Google Wallet. In this live stream, Gokmen will walk us through his day, offering insights into the challenges, achievements, and collaborative spirit involved in building and maintaining Google Wallet. Don't miss this unique opportunity to ask Gokmen your questions live!

Subscribe to Google for Developers → https://goo.gle/developers
Subscribe to the Google Wallet Developer Newsletter → https://g.co/wallet/newsletter

Speaker: Edson Yanaga
Products Mentioned: Google Wallet

entry.title: Get started with Gemma 3 on Vertex AI

group.title: Get started with Gemma 3 on Vertex AI
group.description:
Simplify AI model deployment and tuning with Vertex AI! This video demonstrates how to use Vertex AI Model Garden to deploy Gemma 3 in just three lines of code. Plus, learn how to fine-tune Gemma 3 using Vertex AI tools and infrastructure, unlocking powerful AI capabilities for your applications.

Chapters:
0:00 - Introduction
2:07 - How to leverage Gemma 3 models effectively
3:11 - Deployment Demo
5:50 - Tuning demo

Resources:
Model Garden →  https://goo.gle/44zhWt4 
Gemma documentation → https://goo.gle/406JjIM

Subscribe to Google for Developers → https://goo.gle/developers 

Speaker: Ivan Nardini. Erwin Huizenga
Products Mentioned: Gemma, Vertex AI

entry.title: Build a Browser Use Agent with ADK and Selenium

group.title: Build a Browser Use Agent with ADK and Selenium
group.description:
Elevate your retail website's performance with a Browser Use agent, built with Agent Development Kit and Selenium, to enhance product data. This powerful tool generates relevant keywords from your product information, then actively visits your site, analyzes top search results, and provides actionable suggestions to enrich your product titles for better visibility. By identifying and filling gaps in your product data, the agent helps ensure your website users always find products by your brand. 

Recources: 
Brand Search Optimization → https://goo.gle/42XZgRG 
Get Started → https://goo.gle/3RGrB9T 
Other Sample Agents →https://goo.gle/3Rbdo4s 

Chapters; 
0:00 - Intro 
1:13 - Chapter 1: Brand search optimization workflow
2:30 - Chapter 2: Architecture
7:53 - Chapter 3: Setup the agent with bigquery
9:55 - Chapter 4: Root Agent and sub agent walkthrough
14:44 - Chapter 5: Web browser / Search results Agent
22:39 - Chapter 6: Evaluation and Testing
27:20 - Chapter 7: Running the ADK web app
35:57 - Chapter 8: Recap

Subscribe to Google for Developers → https://goo.gle/developers 

#BrandSearchOptimization #ProductData #SEO

entry.title: What’s the minimum total distance the train must travel to deliver all cars? Go!

group.title: What’s the minimum total distance the train must travel to deliver all cars? Go!
group.description:
Here's an algorithmic challenge with real-world applications: A train must deliver specific numbers of cargo cars to three stations with constraints on capacity. How would you optimize the delivery route to minimize total distance traveled? What's your minimum-distance solution, and what approach did you use to find it? Share your thoughts in the comments!

Subscribe to Google for Developers → https://goo.gle/developers 

Speakers: M.E Francis

group.title: Deep Dive into Long Context
group.description:
Explore the synergy between long context models and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) in this episode of the Release Notes podcast. Join Google DeepMind's Nikolay Savinov with host Logan Kilpatrick as they discuss scaling context windows into the millions, recent quality improvements, RAG versus long context, and what's next in the field.

Listen to this podcast: 
Apple Podcasts → https://goo.gle/3Bm7QzQ 
Spotify → https://goo.gle/3ZL3ADl 

Chapters:
0:00 - Intro 
0:52 Introduction & defining tokens
5:27 Context window importance
9:53 RAG vs. Long Context
14:19 Scaling beyond 2 million tokens
18:41 Long context improvements since 1.5 Pro release
23:26 Difficulty of attending to the whole context
28:37 Evaluating long context: beyond needle-in-a-haystack
33:41 Integrating long context research
34:57 Reasoning and long outputs
40:54 Tips for using long context
48:51 The future of long context: near-perfect recall and cost reduction
54:42 The role of infrastructure
56:15 Long-context and agents

Subscribe to Google for Developers → https://goo.gle/developers 


Speaker: Nikolay Savinov, Logan Kilpatrick
Products Mentioned:  Google AI,

entry.title: Customize Gemma with Hugging Face Transformers

group.title: Customize Gemma with Hugging Face Transformers
group.description:
